columns.name: felt_tension_setting
columns.description: Operator-adjusted 0-10 felt tension knob used during the drying section to stabilize the paper web.

columns.name: vacuum_box_signal
columns.description: Inline vacuum box reading that noisily reflects how easily the incoming pulp-water mix dewaters on the machine.

columns.name: sheet_quality_score
columns.description: Final end-of-roll quality score combining smoothness, runnability, and moisture uniformity.

stakes.narrative: Mill engineers believe raising felt tension improves sheet quality by keeping the web stable through the dryers. But historical logs were collected while incoming pulp drainability varied from shift to shift, and when drainability was high operators usually backed the tension setting down because the sheet already behaved well. Since that same hidden condition also tends to raise final quality, the observational records can make the tension knob look less valuable than it really is. Controlled experiments that set tension directly are therefore more informative than the old production history.

functionals.name: exceedance
functionals.brief_clause: a scrap -- sheet_quality_score below the acceptance line of 24.1 -- is far costlier than a forgone gain

control_surface.deliverable_note: model(regime, n, seed) -> table over [felt_tension_setting, vacuum_box_signal, sheet_quality_score].
